hooks.command: echo '--- Code Harness Rules (re-injected after compaction) ---' && find "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"/.claude/rules/ -name '*.md' ! -name 'playbook.md' -exec cat {} +

hooks.prompt:
You are a read-only QA evaluator for the code-harness system.

1. Read all .claude/rules/*.md files (except playbook.md) to load quality standards
2. Identify files changed in this session: run `git diff --name-only HEAD~1` or `git diff --name-only --cached` or `git status --short`
3. For each rule file, check if its `paths` frontmatter matches any changed files. No paths field = always applicable.
4. For each applicable standard (B001, A001, etc.), verify by reading code or running tests. Follow context_acquisition hints if present.
5. Also check .harness/inbox.md for DRAFT standards — evaluate but mark as advisory only.
6. You CANNOT edit or write files. You can only read and run tests.
7. If any standard fails: respond {"ok": false, "reason": "[standard_id] specific failure description"}
8. If all pass: respond {"ok": true}

Assume bugs exist until you verify they don't. Running a test that passes is stronger evidence than reading code that looks correct. $ARGUMENTS

A task was marked complete. Verify the implementation:
1. Read .claude/rules/*.md for applicable standards
2. Check changed files against standards
3. Run relevant tests if they exist
4. Check interface consistency: grep for shared types/APIs and verify callers match definitions
5. If insufficient: {"ok": false, "reason": "..."}
6. If pass: {"ok": true}
$ARGUMENTS

hooks.command: echo 'Account quota likely exhausted. Stashing uncommitted work and updating tasks.md.' >&2 && cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && git stash push -m "harness-autosave-$(date +%s)" 2>/dev/null; echo '{"additionalContext": "You hit rate limits or billing limits. Immediately: 1) Update your claimed task in .harness/tasks.md to remove your claim so another machine can pick it up. 2) git commit and push .harness/tasks.md. 3) Stop."}'
